title: Reinventing Entropy | Compression is Intelligence Part 1
summary: A video essay visualizing entropy and information theory through the lens of compression. The work explores how compression relates to intelligence using Shannon's mathematical theory of communication and demonstrates how language compressibility reflects information content.
writeup: This video opens the first of a trilogy grounding the claim that compression is intelligence by walking through Shannon's noiseless coding theorem from first principles. The central through-line is that prediction and compression are mathematically equivalent, which reframes LLM pre-training from 'next-token prediction' into 'building the most efficient text compressor,' giving the cross-entropy loss objective a concrete information-theoretic interpretation. The exposition builds from a toy robot-navigation puzzle, discovers prefix-free codes and the probability-to-bit-length alignment, and arrives at entropy as the natural quantity that characterises the fundamental compression limit.
key_points: Shannon's 1940s information theory and modern LLM pre-training share the same mathematics: the cross-entropy loss used to train large language models has its roots in information theory, and the noiseless coding theorem implies that next-token prediction and optimal compression are two descriptions of the same objective.

A prefix-free (prefix code) encoding assigns shorter bit strings to more probable symbols, achieving 1.75 bits per instruction for a four-symbol distribution (1/2, 1/4, 1/8, 1/8) versus the naive flat 2-bit encoding, by ensuring no codeword is a prefix of another so decoding is unambiguous.

The founding insight of information theory is the exact alignment between probabilities and code-space fractions: assigning a codeword of length L to a symbol consumes exactly 1/2^L of the available prefix-free code space, which matches the symbol's probability when the code is optimal.

A perfect compression algorithm would, by necessity, produce output indistinguishable from random noise, because any detectable pattern implies residual compressibility, making incompressibility the theoretical ceiling and motivating the formal definition of entropy.

The video frames entropy and information not as arbitrary definitions to memorise but as quantities that are inescapably forced on you once you try to characterise the hard limit of compression, building intuition before the formula.
